title: Kage Architecture & Components
summary: Kage is an agent memory system built on a 3 tier architecture: Project, Personal, and Global. Component Overview Data Flows 1. Memory Capture : When a bug is fixed, kage distiller writes a node to pending/ . 2. Review Cy
body:
Kage is an agent memory system built on a 3-tier architecture: Project, Personal, and Global.

## Component Overview
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Claude Code Session] -->|SessionStart| B(Load Tiers & KI)
    A -->|Main Task| C{Make Decision?}
    C -->|Yes| D[kage-memory sub-agent]
    D --> E[.agent_memory/ project]
    D --> F[~/.agent_memory/ personal]
    D --> G[kage-graph global HTTP]
    
    A -->|Task Complete| H[kage-distiller]
    H --> I[.agent_memory/pending/]
    
    I -->|User /kage review| E
```

## Data Flows
1. **Memory Capture**: When a bug is fixed, `kage-distiller` writes a node to `pending/`.
2. **Review Cycle**: The user runs `/kage review` to approve the node, moving it to `nodes/`.
3. **Retrieval**: Before making a domain decision, `kage-memory` searches the project index first, then personal, then falls back to the global CDN via `kage-graph`.

## Connections
- **hooks-system** (`integrates`): Driven by the `Stop`, `SessionStart`, and `PostToolUse` shell scripts.
